items.title: [正面] Recently, the developer of this game has been send...
items.summary:
Recently, the developer of this game has been sending out in-game mail to players in which he talks about the game's future, including challenges securing funding, but then he announced that funding for the immediate future had been found, and he thanked the players for their unwavering support during what must have been a rather stressful period for him.

When it comes to gacha games my personal philosophy, shared by many I hope, is never to spend what you are unwilling or cannot afford to lose.  Gacha games close all the time; eventually the content dries up or can no longer hold the interest of its players, who move on to greener pastures.  

The dev could have been engaging in the "everything must go" pitch by making it seem like the game was on the virtual edge of closing in an effort to get players to spend more...but I kind of doubt it: the last thing a gacha game wants is to make players cautious about spending their money.  I'd like to think the dev was simply making an effort to be transparent about what was going on with the game, but it honestly doesn't matter since I'd rather talk about Morimens.

First thing you need to know is that this game does not have a proper English localization; ChatGPT is employed to handle the work of translating Chinese into English and it's by no means perfect, but it is very impressive how serviceable the translations are.  If you're thinking of trying this game out, just be aware that you're going to need to use your brain a bit to work through any confusing bits of translation; it isn't really hard to figure out.

The second thing you need to know is that Morimens is a gacha title.  For those unaware of the term "gacha", it stems from the Japanese term "gachapon" which refers to any type of collectible system, digital or physical, where the customer has no idea what item in that respective system they're purchasing (i.e the item is concealed until you open it after purchasing, somewhat in the sense of how "grab bags" work).  Arguments abound about whether this constitutes gambling, and I have to say, yeah: it is definitely gambling.  As stated above, don't spend what you can't afford to lose because trying to pull (i.e gamble) for a specific character ("Awaker") or its respective artifact ("Wheel of Destiny") can be an exercise in futility and frustration, both for you and your wallet.  Thankfully, the game is quite generous with rewarding players with the means to make many free pulls; judicious management of these free resources will allow even the most tight-fisted spender to experience everything Morimens has to offer without spending a dime.

The third thing about Morimens to know is that the game is primarily set in an alternate England (setting much like Lovecraft's Arkham) with heavy themes of both steampunk and Lovecraft (one of the founders of the cosmic horror genre).  Blood, ichor, horrific body modification, forced medical experimentation, insanity, death, loss, and the horror of the known becoming strange are all on display here, so if you are bothered by any of it, you should think twice before sallying forth to Mythag (the name of the central organization and school of the game, an alternate Miskatonic University from Lovecraft).  
In this setting, the Cthulic pantheon exists, from the Outer Ones to the Great Old Ones, and even the Elder Gods walk in the space between both stars and thought.  

If you're new to this setting, it may be helpful to read "Cthulu Mythos" on Wikipedia as well as any unknown creatures and concept words that the article mentions; you don't have to be familiar with the stories set in the Cthulu universe but it does help the player get a better understanding of both the game and the context to help parse any odd ChatGPT translations.

The gameplay consists of stages ("investigations") that combine story elements with turn-based combat using a randomized card draw system: each Awaker (up to 4) contributes 5-6 cards to the deck where each card represents either a special ability of the respective Awaker, a basic attack ("strike"), or a basic defend.  There are also various "Symptom" cards that arise from certain choices, enemy attacks, and consequences which will be forced in your deck so that you must deal with them accordingly.  Awakers build up a power gauge ("Alimus") when the player plays their respective cards and, when this gauge is full, can unleash a special action ("Exaltation") which cause powerful effects in combat.  The ability to play these cards is regulated by a resource akin to mana in more traditional RPGs called ("Arithmetica"; will revisit) which is replenished each turn and is spent on the casting cost of the chosen card to play.

All in all the game is quite fun but, like most gacha games, do require a considerable investment of time in order to procure various resources to gradually empower your characters.  The gameplay is a decently fun loop, the characters have well-defined, if two-dimensional, personalities and motives, and the story is rather dark, brooding, and melancholy, but what else could be expected?

If after reading all this, you're curious about the game, then install it!  The game is free to both download and to play; in fact, I would strongly discourage anyone from spending money on this at least until they decide they enjoy the game and are more or less wanting to help support it with their money.

items.title:
[正面] 以免費遊戲內容來說很不錯，上手後可以說是很有趣
不過前提是你要能夠忍受以下幾點「小問題」
首先在大門...
items.summary:
以免費遊戲內容來說很不錯，上手後可以說是很有趣
不過前提是你要能夠忍受以下幾點「小問題」
首先在大門口你得點好幾次才能「進入大學」，真不知道這大學是不是在勸退我們別來
接著當你丟完牌開始跑各種動畫時，切換視窗再切回來就得忍受各種延遲性一次爆發轟炸你耳朵的各種聲音，原本我很喜歡聽角色語音的，這種爆炸聲音搞到我好幾次都得把音量關掉才能玩得下去
還有遊戲內各種特別但毫無指引解釋的「專有名詞」，它們簡直比元素週期表還要難懂，升級材料也很難取得，我到現在還是不懂一個禮拜只能刷一個升技能材料，這樣我技能要農到民國幾年才能升一等？
暫時只能想到這些了，聽說這遊戲打贏復活賽要前進2.0了，只希望能先修好一些顯而易見的bug，一直推新角新劇情這種bug都不修我連月卡都不太想課了
問我好不好玩，是好玩的
但問我願不願意推薦，我是不推的
